Grimes
Off Calendar May-05-2021 Continued To Jun-16-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Fees
Off Calendar Jun-16-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa, via ZOOM, counsel for Bennett Hong, Trustee; Rowena Navia, via ZOOM, counsel for Michael Torres, Petitioner.

At the outset, the court queries Ms. Navia regarding whether there has been sufficient notice of her petition to other creditors. Ms. Navia believes that no additional creditors remain to notify. Ms. Navia also reports that she believes that she can obtain waivers of notice from the three remaining parties and will submit them to the court as soon as she has them and requests a brief continuance to do so. Waivers of Notice should state that copies of the petition have been received. Mr. Siracusa respectfully requests that if Ms. Navia's request to continue her petition is granted, that the court grant his petition to avoid the need for him to appear. The court then asks if anyone objects to the court granting Mr. Siracusa's petition, no objections are reported.

After additional discussion, the Second and Final Account and Report of Successor Trustee and Petition for its Settlement, etc., is taken under submission. The Petition for Legal Fees and Costs, etc., is continued to June 16, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Fees
Off Calendar Mar-29-2021 Continued To May-05-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa appeared by zoom for Bennett Hong (Trustee).
Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for Michael Torres (Petitioner for the appointment of Bennett Hong as Successor Trustee).

The Court asked Mr. Siracusa if he is willing to have his Client's petition heard with Mr. Torres' petition. Mr. Siracusa stated that he is willing to do so, but there are limited funds to pay for all of the fees that have been requested, that they have an agreement that they won't pay any of his fees until the other petition is heard and that the Court can grant his petition. The Court stated that it will put everything together. The Second and Final Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. is continued to May 5, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Nov-04-2020 Continued To Dec-15-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust Provision
Off Calendar Oct-20-2020 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Oct-08-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa, Esq., counsel for Bennett Hong, Trustee (appearing by video conference).

The Court notes certain concerns in the current accounting, primarily regarding the fees of the conservator and those of counsel. While this is a trust accounting, there appears to be time better attributable to the conservatorship, with the Court noting that it was the Court's understanding that Mr. Hong had waived his fees as conservator. Mr. Siracusa offers explanation, noting difficulties collecting and then accounting for a Citibank account and the Conservatee's pension. As it was understood at the inception of the conservatorship that most of the estate assets would be marshalled in the trust, the accounting here does, in fact, account for some conservatorship time that rightfully should have been trust time had the marshalling of the aforementioned assets not been otherwise problematic.

Court and counsel discuss paralegal hourly billing rates as evidenced in the accounting.
The Court sets hearing on status of the final accounting for January 5, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. If counsel has filed the Petition by that date, no appearance is necessary on January 5th. The Court takes the current matter under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Johnson. (D204)

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Bennett Hong (Trustee).

The Court noted that the Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement with the Tenant has been granted. Mr. Siracusa reported that the tenant is still living in the property (the basement), that they can't pay the settlement until the house is sold and that the tenant needs the money so that she can move. The sale was confirmed at $600,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $148,000.00 is required.

Off Calendar May-08-2019 Continued To May-22-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Bring Trust Under Court Supervision And The Appointment Of Bennett Hong As General Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-30-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia for Michael Torres.
Nancy Rasch Court appointed for James Grimes (Conservatee).

Ms. Rasch stated that she was appointed as Counsel for Mr. Grimes in the Conservatorship, but she was not formerly appointed in the trust matter. Ms. Navia stated that she believes Ms. Rasch's involvement is integral, that the conservatorship matter really deals with many of the same issues, that they have been trying to get a copy of the original trust and that she anticipates needing to do a petition for substituted judgment. The Court stated that it is certainly appropriate that Ms. Rasch's involvement include issues concerning the Conservatee's estate planning concerns and that if Ms. Rasch wants something formal, she can file a drop off ex parte petition. The Court waives any problems with notice due to Court continuances and noted that potential objectors are not here. Ms. Navia stated that she has not heard from anyone. Ms. Rasch stated that she got a call from an Attorney who was considering representing Willy Tupoula, that Willy and Terry are in the house, that the Conservatee is temporarily out of State, that he seems happy and that he does not seem interested in returning. Ms. Rasch also stated that she feels it is a good thing for Mr. Grimes to stay where he is while the Trustee does something to improve the condition of the house. Ms. Navia stated that bond should be set at $70,000.00 and that if/when the property is sold they will put in a request to increase bond. The Court granted the Petition to Bring Trust Under Court Supervision and the Appointment of Bennett Hong as Successor Trustee (subject to review of the order). The real property may not be encumberred or sold without prior Court order. Status on filing of bond is set for May 8, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of an accounting is set for June 16, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
